skill.name: excalidraw-diagram-generator
skill.description: Generate Excalidraw diagrams from natural language descriptions, including flowcharts, architecture diagrams, mind maps, data flow diagrams, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, and ER diagrams.
skill.summary: Create editable Excalidraw diagrams from text prompts and structured diagram requirements.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are false positives from Markdown code fences, Excalidraw schema examples, cloud service names, and documentation URLs. The real concern is that bundled Python helpers read, rename, and write user-supplied diagram or library paths, which is legitimate for the skill but should be used only on trusted local files. No evidence found of malicious network access, credential access, command execution in Python code, or prompt injection text.

medium_findings.title: Local File Modification Helpers Require Trusted Paths
medium_findings.description: The bundled Python helpers write Excalidraw files and generated icon references using paths supplied at runtime. This is expected functionality, but an agent should not run these scripts against sensitive or untrusted paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write calls are direct and confirmed in executable Python scripts. The risk is moderate because the behavior is part of the documented diagram editing workflow and no malicious target path is hardcoded.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged As Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are Markdown examples or inline schema fields, not runtime shell execution. The executable Python files reviewed do not invoke subprocesses or shells.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation code fences or type examples. I found no evidence at these locations of actual command execution by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Flagged As Network Activity
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to Excalidraw source metadata or user-facing setup instructions. No reviewed Python script performs HTTP requests or uploads data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or Excalidraw file source values. There is no network client code at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: Cloud Icon Names And Random IDs Flagged As Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: C2 and weak cryptography detections appear to match terms such as EC2 plus non-security random ID examples. These are diagramming concepts and identifier examples, not command-and-control logic or cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited EC2 and random value examples are used for diagrams and element IDs. No evidence found that they implement C2 behavior or security-sensitive cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Supplied Path File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Helper scripts accept diagram or library paths from command-line arguments and write JSON or Markdown output. This requires path discipline when agents call the scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly derive paths from argv and write to those paths. The pattern is dangerous only if invoked with sensitive or attacker-controlled paths.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear diagrams without spending time on manual drawing. This skill turns structured prompts into editable Excalidraw files for processes, systems, data models, and ideas.
actual_capabilities: Generates .excalidraw files that can be opened in Excalidraw.
Creates flowcharts, relationship diagrams, mind maps, architecture diagrams, data flow diagrams, class diagrams, sequence diagrams, and ER diagrams.
Uses bundled templates for common diagram types and layout patterns.
Documents required Excalidraw element properties, including coordinates, colors, arrows, and text settings.
Provides helper scripts for adding arrows and icons from local Excalidraw libraries.
Can split local .excalidrawlib files into reusable icon JSON files and a reference table.
limitations: Generated diagrams still depend on clear user input and may need manual layout adjustments.
Icon support requires users to provide compatible local Excalidraw library files.
The helper scripts modify local files and should be run only on trusted diagram paths.
Very large diagrams should be split into smaller diagrams for readability.

use_cases.title: Map a Product Workflow
use_cases.description: Create a flowchart that shows user steps, decision points, and handoffs in a product experience.

use_cases.title: Draft a System Architecture
use_cases.description: Visualize services, databases, queues, cloud components, and data movement in an editable Excalidraw file.

use_cases.title: Design a Data Model
use_cases.description: Build an ER or class diagram that captures entities, attributes, relationships, and cardinality.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Flowchart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short process diagram.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excalidraw flowchart for user registration with start, email entry, email verification, password setup, and completion.

prompt_templates.title: Relationship Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for entities and labeled links.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excalidraw relationship diagram for Users, Posts, Comments, and Tags. Show the main relationships with labels.

prompt_templates.title: System Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for technical components and data flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excalidraw architecture diagram for a web app with browser, CDN, API service, worker queue, database, cache, and object storage.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-View Design Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs multiple diagram types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise diagram package for an order management system: one architecture diagram, one sequence diagram for checkout, and one ER diagram for core tables.

output_examples.input: Create a flowchart for a support ticket lifecycle.
output_examples.output: An editable Excalidraw flowchart with intake, triage, assignment, investigation, resolution, and closure steps connected by arrows.

output_examples.input: Diagram an AWS-style web application architecture.
output_examples.output: An Excalidraw architecture diagram with labeled cloud components, network boundaries, service connections, and optional local icon integration.

output_examples.input: Create an ER diagram for a blog platform.
output_examples.output: An Excalidraw data model diagram showing users, posts, comments, tags, primary keys, foreign keys, and relationship cardinality.

best_practices: Provide the diagram type, key elements, relationships, and desired level of detail in the prompt.
Keep one diagram focused on one workflow, system view, or data model.
Use local icon libraries only from trusted sources and confirm licenses before redistribution.
anti_patterns: Requesting a large system map without naming the main components or boundaries.
Using one diagram for unrelated workflows, infrastructure, and database design.
Running helper scripts on sensitive paths or downloaded libraries from untrusted sources.

faq.question: What file format does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates .excalidraw files that can be opened and edited in Excalidraw.

faq.question: Which diagram types are supported?
faq.answer: It supports flowcharts, relationship diagrams, mind maps, architecture diagrams, data flow diagrams, swimlanes, class diagrams, sequence diagrams, and ER diagrams.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Core diagram generation does not require internet access. Optional icon setup may direct users to download libraries separately.

faq.question: Can it use AWS or cloud icons?
faq.answer: Yes, if a compatible Excalidraw icon library is available locally and has been split with the bundled script.

faq.question: Are generated diagrams ready to present?
faq.answer: They are useful drafts, but complex diagrams may need manual spacing, grouping, or visual refinement.

faq.question: What safety precautions should users follow?
faq.answer: Run helper scripts only on trusted local diagram files and review downloaded icon libraries before processing them.
